steps.title: Read two clear points from the graphed line
steps.content: The graph shows $y = f(x) + 19$. Reading directly from the grid: at $x = 0$, $y = 4$; at $x = 2$, $y = 0$.

steps.title: Calculate the slope of the graphed line
steps.content:
Using the two points $(0, 4)$ and $(2, 0)$:
$$\text{slope} = \frac{0 - 4}{2 - 0} = -2$$The line has a negative slope, ruling out choices C and D (which have $+cx$ form).

steps.title: Write the equation of $y = f(x) + 19$ and isolate $f(x)$
steps.content:
The line has slope $-2$ and y-intercept \$4$:
$$y = -2x + 4$$
Substitute into the definition $y = f(x) + 19$:
$$f(x) + 19 = -2x + 4$$
$$f(x) = -2x - 15$$
Rewrite as $f(x) = -15 - 2x = -d - cx$ where $c = 2$ and $d = 15$ (both positive constants). That matches choice A — A is correct.
